channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Sanctions and Oil Blockade Impact Life in Cuba
item.description: The article explores the impact of escalating US sanctions and an oil blockade on Cuba, detailing how these measures have crippled essential services, caused widespread blackouts, and severely disrupt...

item.title: JICA Focuses on Youth and Women's Economic Growth
item.description: The Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA) has launched new initiatives to support economic empowerment for youth and women, aiming to reduce inequality and promote sustainable growth. These pr...

item.title: Epstein Inquiry Faces Criticism for Lack of Progress After One Year
item.description: The U.S. House Oversight Committee's inquiry into Jeffrey Epstein, now in its first year, has faced significant criticism for failing to produce actionable results. High-profile witnesses including bi...

item.title: Kooij triumphs in chaotic Tour de France stage after crash
item.description: Dutch cyclist Olav Kooij secured a dramatic victory in the fifth stage of the Tour de France after a chaotic sprint finish marred by a crash. The 24-year-old rider from the Decathlon CMA CGM Team edge...

item.title: Himalayan Glacial Melt Alters Downstream Water Chemistry
item.description: A study on Rulung Glacier in Ladakh reveals that rising temperatures are intensifying interactions between meltwater and rocks, altering the chemical composition of water flowing into the Indus river ...

item.title: Delhi Development Authority Launches Portal for Transit-Oriented Development Projects
item.description: The Delhi Development Authority (DDA) has launched a dedicated portal to streamline approvals for transit-oriented development (TOD) projects under the revised policy. This initiative allows developer...

item.title: New Direct Flights Between Tanzania and Moscow Boost Tourism and Trade
item.description: Tanzania's tourism industry is set for a major boost following the launch of Air Tanzania Company Limited's (ATCL) direct flights between Dar es Salaam and Moscow. The new route, secured through Presi...

item.title: Yoruba-Hausa clash sparks panic and property damage in Ibadan
item.description: A violent clash between Yoruba and Hausa groups in Ibadan's Ojoo area caused chaos, with roads blocked, properties set ablaze, and security forces deployed to restore order. Eyewitnesses reported heav...

item.title: Investigations into Former Anti-Corruption Commissioner Discontinued Post-Resignation
item.description: The inspector of the National Anti-Corruption Commission (NACC) has discontinued two investigations into its former commissioner, Paul Brereton, following his resignation in July 2026. Gail Furness, t...

item.title: Posthumous pardon for Ruth Ellis, last woman executed in UK, recognized as abuse victim
item.description: Ruth Ellis, the last woman to be executed in the UK, has been granted a posthumous conditional pardon by King Charles III. The decision, announced by Deputy Prime Minister David Lammy, replaces the de...

item.title: Legal experts dismiss Trump's appeal to block Carroll payment
item.description: Former President Donald Trump's attempt to halt the payment of $5 million to writer E. Jean Carroll following a jury verdict finding him liable for sexual assault and defamation has been met with skep...

item.title: Wildfire Smoke Causes Orange Skies and High Air Quality Alerts in Kamloops
item.description: Wildfire smoke from the Fraser Canyon has created unusually vivid orange skies over Kamloops, British Columbia, with air quality classified as 'very high risk' by Environment Canada. Residents describ...

item.title: Solar Dimming Could Mitigate El Niño Impacts, Study Suggests
item.description: A new study proposes that dimming the sun through marine cloud brightening (MCB) could reduce the severity of El Niño events and their global impacts. El Niño, a natural climate phenomenon in the Paci...

item.title: Insights from a WSJ Article: Carney's Davos Speech and Trump-Trudeau Call
item.description: The Wall Street Journal article highlights key developments in Canada-US relations, focusing on Prime Minister Mark Carney's efforts to navigate tensions with former President Donald Trump. Carney rep...

item.title: Aviation Unions Demand Payment of Unpaid Ticket Levies to Avoid Strike
item.description: Aviation unions in Nigeria have issued a 14-day ultimatum to airline operators, demanding immediate payment of the statutory five percent Ticket Sales Charge (TSC) collected from passengers. The union...

item.title: Two Men Killed in Road Accident Near Bagalkot
item.description: Two men, Manjunath Meti (26) and his nephew Shreeshail Bavi (24), died in a severe road accident near Sunaga in Bagalkot district on July 8, 2026. Their bike collided with a truck on the Bilagi-Bagalk...

item.title: Actors Brittany Allen and Jeff Kober Self-Submit for Emmy Noms After Being Overlooked by HBO Max
item.description: Brittany Allen and Jeff Kober, guest stars of HBO Max’s medical drama 'The Pitt,' secured Emmy nominations after self-submitting their performances when the network initially excluded them from its of...

item.title: Moana live-action remake criticized for lacklustre performance and heavy reliance on CGI
item.description: The live-action remake of Disney's 2016 animated hit Moana has been met with criticism for its lacklustre execution and overreliance on CGI. While Dwayne Johnson reprises his role as the demigod Maui,...

item.title: The History and Artistry of Fordite: A Byproduct of Industrial Innovation
item.description: Fordite, also known as Detroit agate, is a unique gemstone formed by the accidental accumulation of paint overspray from mid-century automotive factories. Created between the 1920s and 1960s, this mat...

item.title: NSW Wins State of Origin Decider at Suncorp Despite Queensland's Struggles
item.description: New South Wales (NSW) have pulled off a stunning victory in the 2026 State of Origin decider, defeating a disjointed Queensland side at Suncorp Stadium. Led by halfback Nathan Cleary, the Blues overca...
